title: rubus capitatus
intro: Rubus capitatus, also known as the Hawaiian strawberry or strawberry guava, is a shrub-like plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, characterized by its small white flowers and edible red fruits. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Rubus capitatus include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. Rubus capitatus can be prepared as a tea, infused oil, or tincture, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other culinary applications.
